brief: Global conflicts and tensions continue to impact India's economy, with the country's crude oil import bill increasing by 77% in the first quarter of FY27 due to rising international prices. The ongoing US-Iran war and airspace limitations have also reduced international traffic, affecting Delhi's overseas flights and potentially keeping a newly converted international pier at IGI Airport's T3 idle until September. Meanwhile, the RBI is navigating the conflicting external conditions of an energy shock and an AI boom, adopting a wait-and-see approach to policy decisions as India's net FDI inflows are expected to reach $15 billion in FY27, driven by healthy gross inflows and moderating repatriation outflows.

Geopolitics.title: Nods in, but low int’l traffic to keep T3 pier C idle till Sept
Geopolitics.summary: The newly converted international Pier C at IGI Airport's T3 may remain idle until September due to reduced international traffic caused by the US-Iran war and airspace limitations. This reduction in traffic is affecting Delhi's overseas flights. The situation highlights the impact of global conflicts on local economies.

Geopolitics.title: Ontario Wildfires Ignite International Tensions
Geopolitics.summary: Wildfires in Ontario have led to international tensions between Canada and the US, with the US President criticizing Canada's response to the crisis. The situation has also highlighted the climate crisis, with poor air quality affecting both countries. The tensions have sparked a defensive response from Canada's Premier.

Geopolitics.title: War shock lifts India’s Q1 crude bill 77% to Rs 4.64 L-cr
Geopolitics.summary: India's crude oil import bill has increased by 77% in the first quarter of FY27 due to rising international prices caused by the conflict in West Asia and geopolitical tensions. Despite a 4.5% drop in volumes, the bill has soared to Rs 4.64 lakh crore. This increase highlights the impact of global conflicts on India's economy.

Geopolitics.title: Moldova relations poised to enter new era of closer engagement: MEA
Geopolitics.summary: The Ministry of External Affairs has stated that India-Moldova relations are set to enter a new era of closer engagement. This development highlights the expanding diplomatic ties between the two countries. The new era of engagement is expected to strengthen bilateral relations.

Geopolitics.title: Despite Trump-Xi agreement, China’s rare earth magnet exports to US remain 20% below pre-trade war levels
Geopolitics.summary: China's exports of rare-earth magnets to the US remain 20% below pre-trade war levels, despite an agreement between Trump and Xi. This discrepancy highlights the ongoing trade tensions between the two countries. The rare-earth magnet exports are a significant indicator of the trade relationship between the US and China.

Geopolitics.title: Diplomatic Dance: Potential Meeting Between Rubio and Lavrov at ASEAN Summit
Geopolitics.summary: A potential meeting between US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov may take place at the ASEAN summit in Manila. The meeting would be a significant development in international diplomacy between the US and Russia. Discussions are underway to arrange the diplomatic engagement, highlighting continued efforts in international diplomacy.

Economy.title: Energy shock vs AI boom: RBI should act as and when external shocks push domestic data in a direction, not until then
Economy.summary: The RBI's monetary policy must navigate the conflicting external conditions of an energy shock and an AI boom. The energy shock is driven by conflict, impacting oil prices and shipping routes, while the AI boom supports growth, particularly in the US. The RBI is likely to adopt a wait-and-see approach to policy decisions.

Economy.title: Kerala hikes rubber support price by ₹50/kg in relief for farmers
Economy.summary: The Kerala government has increased the rubber support price by ₹50/kg to alleviate financial pressure on rubber farmers. This move aims to help farmers cope with higher production costs. The new support price is ₹250 per kg.

Economy.title: Climate change is causing structural shifts in India’s inflation dynamics-forecasting must adapt
Economy.summary: Climate change is leading to more frequent supply shocks, which is affecting India's inflation dynamics. The country's inflation targeting framework needs to incorporate climate-driven price instability risks for effective monetary policy action. Other central banks are already taking steps in this direction.

Economy.title: India's net FDI inflows likely to reach $15 bn in FY27
Economy.summary: India's net foreign direct investment (FDI) inflows are expected to reach $15 billion in fiscal year 2027, driven by healthy gross inflows and moderating repatriation outflows. Policy measures and a nuanced FDI framework are also expected to boost foreign investment. This signals a positive outlook for India's capital account.

Economy.title: India’s edible oil industry flags 17-fold surge in Nepal imports, seeks policy review
Economy.summary: The Indian edible oil industry is seeking a policy review due to a 17-fold surge in duty-free refined oil imports from Nepal. This surge has led to negative repercussions for local industries and fiscal health, as significant refining operations have moved beyond India's borders.

Economy.title: RBI Governor flags Iran war, weak monsoon as the biggest risks to India’s inflation and economic growth
Economy.summary: The RBI Governor has identified the Iran war and a weak monsoon as the biggest risks to India's inflation and economic growth. Despite these risks, India's economic fundamentals remain strong, and policymakers will monitor inflationary pressures before considering any monetary policy adjustments.

Business.title: India Inc is returning to banks for money, but retail borrowers aren’t keeping pace
Business.summary: India's largest private banks are seeing a shift in credit demand, with corporate and SME lending driving loan growth, while retail borrowing remains slow. This shift is due to stronger working capital demand, refinancing, and business banking. The slower consumer credit growth may impact banks' funding mix and margins.

Business.title: Selective funding slows startup creation in India
Business.summary: The number of startups founded in India has decreased significantly, with 314 startups founded in the first half of 2026 compared to 3,222 in 2025. This decline is attributed to selective funding, which is affecting the creation of new startups in the country. The data highlights a slowdown in the Indian startup ecosystem.

Business.title: up Ecosystem Could Be an Indirect Winner
Business.summary: The upcoming IPOs of OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX may have a positive impact on India's startup ecosystem. Successful AI listings could revive VC funding, improve exits, and boost deep-tech investment, ultimately strengthening India's long-term AI ambitions. This could lead to increased investment and growth in the Indian startup ecosystem.

Business.title: Emergent Raises Series C at $1.5 Billion Valuation, Becomes Unicorn in a Year of Launch
Business.summary: Emergent, an AI software creation platform, has raised $130 million in Series C funding, valuing the company at $1.5 billion. This makes Emergent a unicorn in just a year of its launch, highlighting the growth potential of AI-based startups. The funding round was led by Creaegis and included participation from several prominent investors.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court orders new SIT into Ram temple donation theft, will monitor probe
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has taken over the investigation into the Ram temple donation theft case and will monitor the probe. The court has ousted the local police from the investigation, indicating a need for a more transparent and independent inquiry. This move aims to ensure a fair and thorough investigation into the embezzlement scam.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court takes exception to meagre compensation for ex-HC judges
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has expressed concern over the low monetary compensation provided to retired chief justices and judges of high courts. The court's intervention highlights the need for adequate compensation for judges, considering their significant contributions to the judiciary. This move may lead to a review of the compensation structure for retired judges.

National Affairs.title: Can't force parents to create Apaar IDs for students: Supreme Court
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that parents cannot be forced to create Apaar IDs for their children, which are unique identifiers for students. This decision affects around 24 crore students and emphasizes the importance of protecting individual rights and freedoms. The court's ruling may have implications for the implementation of such identification systems in the future.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court seeks more exclusive UAPA, NDPS courts to speed up trials
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has suggested the establishment of more exclusive courts to handle cases under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA) and the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act. This move aims to expedite the trial process and address the issue of long incarceration periods for accused individuals. The court's recommendation may lead to reforms in the judicial system to ensure faster and more efficient trials.

National Affairs.title: Indus Waters Treaty needs complete renegotiation, says government; blames Pakistan for stonewalling talks
National Affairs.summary: The Indian government has stated that the Indus Waters Treaty needs to be completely renegotiated due to Pakistan's inaction and failure to utilize its water share effectively. The government also expressed concerns over Pakistan's management of the treaty's arbitration framework. The renegotiation is sought in light of changing climate and demographic realities, which may impact the treaty's effectiveness.

The standardization of clauses may benefit policyholders and improve the insurance claims process.

This decision highlights the importance of maintaining social harmony and upholding the rule of law.

This move raises concerns about internet freedom and the government's power to restrict online access.

The court has criticized the council's stance on opposing the enrolment of a law graduate as a lawyer due to a pending criminal case. This development highlights the need for improved regulation and standards in legal education.

brief: Today's brief highlights the interconnectedness of global events and their impact on India's economy and international relations. The ongoing conflict between Ukraine and Russia, as well as the Iran war, are posing significant risks to global trade and oil supplies, which in turn are affecting India's inflation and economic growth prospects. The war between Sudan's army and paramilitary groups has displaced millions, and international aid efforts are hampered by financial shortages and logistical challenges. This crisis is worsening the humanitarian situation in the country.

Geopolitics.title: War and economic crisis complicate efforts to protect Iran's endangered wildlife
Geopolitics.summary: Iran's endangered wildlife populations are being severely impacted by war and economic crises, with conservationists facing challenges in preserving biodiversity. Smuggling and habitat degradation are further endangering species, and international sanctions are restricting funding for conservation organizations. Despite these obstacles, conservation groups continue to work to protect Iran's wildlife.

Geopolitics.title: Iran War: Why Russia’s ‘Doomsday Plane’ Is So Special? Know Features
Geopolitics.summary: Russia's 'Doomsday Plane' is a special aircraft built to function during severe emergencies, including a potential nuclear conflict. It serves as an airborne command centre, allowing top leaders to maintain communication and coordinate national defence under extreme conditions. The plane has unique features that enable it to operate in emergency situations.

Economy.title: UK CETA, not quite the king's ransom expected from a landmark trade deal
Economy.summary: The India-Britain CETA trade deal may provide new market opportunities for Indian exporters, but it also poses challenges for small businesses to comply with British regulations and could limit India's policy-making abilities in areas like affordable medications and digital economy. This trade deal has significant implications for India's trade and economic policies. The agreement may impact India's development goals and access to affordable medications.

Economy.title: Economists expect RBI to hold rates steady, rule out near
Economy.summary: Economists expect the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to keep interest rates unchanged in its upcoming Monetary Policy Committee meeting due to a tight operational balancing act. The RBI is likely to maintain the current interest rates, and a rate hike is not expected in the near term. This decision will have implications for the Indian economy and monetary policy.

Economy.title: Citi, others dial back India rate hike calls as inflation expected to stay moderate
Economy.summary: Economists have delayed their expectations for interest rate hikes in India due to moderate inflation, with retail inflation rising to 4.38% in June. Core inflation remains around 4%, suggesting a lower annual average, which may lead the RBI to avoid tightening policy soon. The decision to hike rates will depend on sustained core inflation above 4.5%.

Economy.title: India's CPI inflation may average 5% in FY27; stable rupee, RBI policy to help contain price pressures: SBI Research
Economy.summary: SBI Research predicts that India's CPI inflation will average 5% in FY27, driven by a stable rupee and RBI policy. This forecast suggests that the RBI's policy and a stable rupee will help contain price pressures. The inflation rate is expected to remain moderate, which will have implications for the Indian economy and monetary policy.

Economy.title: Retail inflation may harden in FY27, RBI could hike rates: Crisil
Economy.summary: Crisil predicts that retail inflation in India may increase in FY27 due to elevated fuel costs, input prices, and weather-related risks to food prices. The RBI may consider hiking interest rates if inflationary pressures persist. This forecast suggests that the RBI will closely monitor inflation and may take policy action to control it.

Economy.title: India's retail inflation may average 5.1% in FY27; Crisil sees possibility of 25 bps RBI rate hike
Economy.summary: Crisil expects India's retail inflation to average 5.1% in FY27, driven by higher fuel costs, input prices, and weather-related risks to food prices. This could strengthen India's long-term AI ambitions and reshape global venture capital flows. Successful AI listings may have a positive impact on India's startup ecosystem.

Business.title: SBI Funds Raises Rs 1880 Crore in Pre-IPO Funding
Business.summary: SBI Funds has raised Rs 1880 crore in pre-IPO funding from investors including WhiteOak Capital and 3P India. The funding was raised through a private placement of equity shares at an issue price of Rs 574 per share. This investment will support the company's growth plans.

Business.title: Aukera Raises Rs 90 Cr in Fresh Funding
Business.summary: Aukera, a premium lab-grown diamond jewellery brand, has raised Rs 90 crore in fresh funding from investors including Alteria Capital and InnoVen Capital. The funding will support the company's national expansion plans and strengthen its balance sheet. This is the company's second funding round in less than a year, reflecting its strong growth trajectory.

Business.title: Bank Credit Growth to Ease as Corporates Tap Bond Markets
Business.summary: Bank credit growth is expected to moderate as large corporations increasingly use domestic bond markets and overseas borrowings for funding. This shift is driven by the Reserve Bank of India's policy measures, which make external funding more attractive for businesses. As a result, banks will finance a smaller share of corporate financing needs.

Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics plans to launch an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028, aiming to raise approximately Rs 3,500-4,000 crore. The company will also undertake a pre-IPO funding round to secure Rs 5000 crore for acquisitions, targeting pan-India growth and new diagnostic technologies. This expansion strategy aims to make Neuberg the second-largest player in the diagnostics market.

Business.title: Corporate Paper Issuances Touch 5-yr High
Business.summary: Indian companies raised Rs 2.53 lakh crore through commercial papers in June, a near five-year high. This surge is driven by attractive pricing, seasonal needs, and debt refinancing, with companies shifting from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions have also contributed to favorable conditions.

National Affairs.title: Air India crash: Draft final report will be ready by October, AAIB tells Supreme Court
National Affairs.summary: The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) has informed the Supreme Court that the final report on the Air India crash will be ready by October, aiming to enhance aviation safety. This report is crucial in understanding the cause of the crash and implementing measures to prevent such incidents in the future. The AAIB's investigation is ongoing, and the report's completion is a significant step towards improving air travel safety.

The court's intervention highlights the need for the government to address the concerns raised by Wangchuk and ensure his safety. The case has sparked concerns about the impact of hunger strikes on individuals and the role of the government in responding to such protests.

This development highlights the challenges faced by investigators in determining the cause of the crash. The AAIB's concern emphasizes the need for a fair and unbiased investigation to ensure that the truth is uncovered and that measures are taken to prevent similar incidents in the future.

The guidelines aim to clarify the definition of attempt to rape and ensure that courts, police, and prosecutors follow a standardized approach in handling such cases. This move is expected to improve the handling of sexual offence cases and provide greater clarity for investigators and courts.

The petition aims to ensure that citizens have access to justice at all times, particularly in emergency situations. The court's decision to examine the plea highlights the importance of ensuring that the justice system is accessible and responsive to the needs of citizens.

This decision follows a recent amendment that recognizes internal disfigurement as a form of disability. The move aims to provide support and protection to acid attack survivors who have suffered internal injuries, ensuring they receive the benefits and assistance they need.

Geopolitics.title: Export headwinds are visible in last month’s shipment data but trade deals could help
Geopolitics.summary: India's shipment data shows a wider goods trade deficit amid uncertainty over the Gulf war. The country's international trade pacts are expected to play a crucial role in uplifting goods and service exports. The impact of trade deals on India's exports will be closely monitored.

Geopolitics.title: US invests $25 mn in critical minerals
Geopolitics.summary: The US Department of War has announced a $25 million investment in ReElement Technologies Corp, a company involved in critical minerals. The investment highlights the importance of critical minerals in the US economy and national security. The move is expected to support the development of critical mineral resources in the US.

Economy.title: Economists expect RBI to hold rates steady, rule out near
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to keep key interest rates unchanged in its upcoming Monetary Policy Committee meeting, as economists believe the central bank is locked into a tight operational balancing act. This decision is based on the current economic conditions and inflation rates. The RBI's move will have a significant impact on the country's economy.

Economy.title: Citi, others dial back India rate hike calls as inflation expected to stay moderate
Economy.summary: Economists have delayed their expectations of an interest rate hike in India due to controlled inflation, with retail inflation rising to 4.38% in June. The Reserve Bank of India may not tighten policy soon, and future rate hikes depend on sustained core inflation above 4.5%. This shift in expectations is based on the current inflation trends.

Economy.title: India's CPI inflation may average 5% in FY27; stable rupee, RBI policy to help contain price pressures: SBI Research
Economy.summary: SBI Research has projected India's CPI inflation to average 5% in FY27, supported by a stable rupee and the Reserve Bank of India's policy. This forecast suggests that the RBI's efforts to contain inflation are expected to be effective. The stable rupee and RBI policy will help mitigate price pressures.

Economy.title: Retail inflation may harden in FY27, RBI could hike rates: Crisil
Economy.summary: Crisil has projected that retail inflation in India may increase in FY27, driven by elevated fuel costs and input prices. The Reserve Bank of India will closely monitor inflation and may consider a rate hike if inflationary pressures persist. This could have a significant impact on the country's economy and monetary policy.

Economy.title: India's retail inflation may average 5.1% in FY27; Crisil sees possibility of 25 bps RBI rate hike
Economy.summary: Crisil has forecast that India's retail inflation may average 5.1% in FY27, driven by factors such as elevated fuel costs and higher input prices. The Reserve Bank of India may consider a 25 basis point rate hike if inflationary pressures persist. This forecast suggests that the RBI will closely monitor inflation and adjust policy accordingly.

Economy.title: India's CPI inflation may average 5% in FY27; stable rupee, RBI policy to help contain price pressures: SBI Research
Economy.summary: SBI Research has projected that India's CPI inflation will average 5% in FY27, with the RBI likely to keep interest rates unchanged for an extended period. A stable rupee and improving foreign inflows are expected to help contain imported inflation. This forecast suggests that the RBI's policy will be effective in managing inflation.

Business.title: AI IPO wave: Why India's start-up ecosystem could be an indirect winner
Business.summary: The upcoming IPOs of OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX could benefit India's startup ecosystem by reviving VC funding, improving exits, and boosting deep-tech investment. This could strengthen India's long-term AI ambitions and reshape global venture capital flows. Successful AI listings may have a positive impact on India's startup ecosystem.

Business.title: SBI Funds raises Rs 1880 crore in pre-IPO funding
Business.summary: SBI Funds has raised Rs 1880 crore in pre-IPO funding from investors including WhiteOak Capital and 3P India. The funding was raised through a private placement of equity shares at an issue price of Rs 574 per share. This funding round is a significant development in the company's plans for its IPO.

Business.title: Aukera raises Rs 90 crore in fresh funding
Business.summary: Aukera, a premium lab-grown diamond jewellery brand, has raised Rs 90 crore in fresh funding from investors including Alteria Capital and InnoVen Capital. This funding round comes less than a year after the company raised $15 million in equity funding. The investment reflects the confidence of lenders and investors in Aukera's growth trajectory.

Business.title: Bank credit growth to ease as corporates tap bond markets, overseas funding
Business.summary: Bank credit growth is expected to moderate as large corporations increasingly use domestic bond markets and overseas borrowings for funding. This shift is driven by the Reserve Bank of India's policy measures, which make external funding more attractive for businesses. As a result, banks will finance a smaller share of corporate financing needs.

Business.title: Neuberg readies for IPO in FY28, to seek up to Rs 4,000 cr
Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics plans to launch an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028, aiming to raise approximately Rs 3,500-4,000 crore. The company also plans to secure Rs 5000 crore in pre-IPO funding for acquisitions, targeting pan-India growth and new diagnostic technologies. Neuberg aims to become the second-largest player in the diagnostics market.

Business.title: Corporate Paper issuances touch 5-yr high at Rs 2.53 lakh crore in June
Business.summary: Indian companies raised a record Rs 2.53 lakh crore through commercial papers in June, driven by attractive pricing, seasonal needs, and debt refinancing. This surge reflects a shift from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding, supported by ample investor demand and a stable liquidity environment. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to favorable conditions.

National Affairs.title: SC asks Odisha to decide remission plea of Dara Singh in Staines murder case
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has asked the Odisha government to decide on the remission plea of Dara Singh, who was convicted of murdering Australian missionary Graham Staines and his two sons. The court's decision is significant as it may set a precedent for similar cases. The case has been ongoing for many years and has garnered significant attention

National Affairs.title: 'India's Got Latent' row: SC imposes Rs 3 lakh cost on Samay Raina for 'taking court for a ride'
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has imposed a fine of Rs 3 lakh on comedian Samay Raina and four other comedians for non-compliance with its orders in the 'India's Got Latent' controversy. The court's decision highlights the importance of respecting court orders and the consequences of non-compliance. The case has sparked debate around online comedy and creator accountability

brief: Tensions between the US and Iran continue to escalate, with the US reinstating a blockade on Iranian shipping in the Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz remaining open for a fee, which may impact global oil prices and international relations. Meanwhile, India's economy is feeling the effects of the geopolitical uncertainty, with rising oil prices threatening to increase inflation and interest rates, and the Reserve Bank of India likely to keep policy rates unchanged at least till October. In other news, Indian companies are increasingly turning to domestic bond markets and overseas funding for financing, with bank credit growth expected to slow down as a result, and several companies, including SBI Funds and Aukera, have raised significant funds through private placements and funding rounds.

Geopolitics.title: Iran War: Why Russia’s ‘Doomsday Plane’ Is So Special?
Geopolitics.summary: Russia's 'Doomsday Plane' is a special aircraft designed to function during severe emergencies, including nuclear conflicts, and serves as an airborne command centre for top leaders. It has unique features that enable communication, strategic directives, and national defence coordination under extreme conditions. This plane is significant in the context of global geopolitical tensions.

Geopolitics.title: Iraq PM Ali al-Zaidi meets Donald Trump in Washington today amid US-Iran war
Geopolitics.summary: Iraq's Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi is meeting with US President Donald Trump in Washington amid escalating tensions between the US and Iran. This meeting is significant as Iraq is a key ally of both the US and Iran, and the discussion is expected to focus on the US-Iran conflict and its impact on the region. The meeting may have implications for global geopolitics and trade.

Geopolitics.title: Export headwinds are visible in last month’s shipment data but trade deals could help
Geopolitics.summary: India's export data shows a widening goods trade deficit due to uncertainty over the Gulf war, but trade deals could help uplift goods and service exports. The impact of the US-Iran conflict on global trade is being closely watched, and India's international trade pacts may play a crucial role in mitigating the effects. The situation highlights the interconnectedness of global trade and geopolitics.

Geopolitics.title: US invests $25 mn in critical minerals
Geopolitics.summary: The US Department of War has announced a $25 million investment in ReElement Technologies Corp to develop critical minerals. This investment is significant as it highlights the US's efforts to secure its supply of critical minerals, which are essential for various industries. The move may have implications for global trade and geopolitics, particularly in the context of the US's relations with other countries.

Geopolitics.title: Trump says US reinstates blockade of Iranian shipping, but Strait of Hormuz to remain open
Geopolitics.summary: US President Donald Trump has announced the reinstatement of a blockade on Iranian shipping in the Gulf, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining open for a fee. This decision is expected to escalate tensions between the US and Iran, with potential implications for global oil prices and international relations. The move may also impact the global economy and trade.

Geopolitics.title: Trump's Bold Reinstatement: Guardian of the Hormuz Strait
Geopolitics.summary: US President Donald Trump has reinstated a blockade on Iranian shipping in the Gulf, ensuring the Strait of Hormuz remains open for a fee. This decision undermines previous attempts at peace and may impact global oil prices and international relations. The move highlights the ongoing tensions between the US and Iran, with significant implications for global geopolitics and trade.

Economy.title: India bonds join global rout after Mideast escalation jolts oil higher
Economy.summary: India's government bonds declined due to rising oil prices caused by fresh Gulf strikes, which may increase India's inflation and interest rate risks. Foreign investors sold Indian bonds, and the rupee also dropped, reflecting concerns over the escalating geopolitical situation. This surge in oil prices threatens to impact India's economy.

Economy.title: Retail Inflation up to 4.4% on costlier food, transport
Economy.summary: India's retail inflation reached 4.4% in June, surpassing the central bank's target, driven by higher food and transport costs. Food inflation accelerated to 5.3%, and core inflation moderated to 3.9%. Economists anticipate the Reserve Bank of India will maintain its current policy rate.

Economy.title: RBI likely to keep rates unchanged till October, India's GDP to moderate to 6.6
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is likely to keep policy rates unchanged at least till October 2026, while India's economy is expected to grow at a steady pace of 6.6-6.8 per cent in FY27 despite global uncertainties. This forecast is based on Bank of Baroda's FY27 Economic Outlook.

Economy.title: E20 petrol may reduce mileage by 3-5%, says Oil Ministry
Economy.summary: The introduction of E20 fuel, which contains 20% ethanol, may reduce vehicle efficiency by 3-5%, but offers benefits like cleaner engines and lower carbon emissions. The Oil Ministry has conducted extensive testing and found that E20 fuel can provide several benefits despite the potential reduction in mileage.

Economy.title: RBI rate hike: Interest burden for Indians may go up later this year amid inflation risks
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to keep policy rates steady in the short term, but rising concerns over domestic inflation may lead to increased rates in the future. Revisions to FY27 economic growth projections indicate a stronger demand trend, while external accounts are anticipated to show improvement with a reduced current account deficit.

Business.title: IPO funding from WhiteOak Capital, 3P India, others
Business.summary: SBI Funds has raised Rs 1880 crore in pre-IPO funding from investors including WhiteOak Capital and 3P India. The funding was raised through a private placement of equity shares at an issue price of Rs 574 per share. This investment will likely support the company's growth plans ahead of its IPO.

Business.title: Aukera Raises Rs. 90 Cr in Fresh Funding
Business.summary: Aukera, a premium lab-grown diamond jewellery brand, has raised Rs 90 crore in fresh funding led by Alteria Capital and other investors. This funding round comes less than a year after the company raised $15 million in equity funding. The investment will support Aukera's national expansion plans.

Business.title: Bank credit growth to ease as corporates tap bond markets, overseas funding
Business.summary: Bank credit growth is expected to slow down as large corporations increasingly use domestic bond markets and overseas borrowings for funding. This shift is driven by the Reserve Bank of India's policy measures, which make external funding more attractive for businesses. As a result, banks will finance a smaller share of corporate financing needs.

Business.title: Neuberg readies for IPO in FY28, to seek up to Rs 4,000 cr
Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics plans to launch an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028, aiming to raise up to Rs 4,000 crore. The company will also undertake a pre-IPO funding round to secure Rs 500 crore for acquisitions, targeting pan-India growth and new diagnostic technologies. Neuberg aims to become the second-largest player in the diagnostics market.

Business.title: Corporate Paper issuances touch 5-yr high at Rs 2.53 lakh crore in June
Business.summary: Indian companies raised a record Rs 2.53 lakh crore through commercial papers in June, driven by attractive pricing, seasonal needs, and debt refinancing. This surge reflects a shift from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding, supported by ample investor demand and a stable liquidity environment. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to favorable conditions.

Business.title: India's banks are handing out more loans, but deposits aren't keeping up
Business.summary: Indian banks have seen a surge in lending, outpacing deposit growth and widening the funding gap. This trend, driven by robust demand from corporate and retail sectors, has pushed the loan-to-deposit ratio to a decade-high, raising concerns about funding sustainability. Banks are adopting varied strategies to manage this imbalance amidst intense competition.

National Affairs.title: From Good Touch To Consent: Sex Education May Soon Become Part Of School Curriculum
National Affairs.summary: The Centre has agreed to introduce comprehensive sex education in schools and colleges across India, which could lead to a significant change in the country's education system. This move aims to provide students with a better understanding of consent and healthy relationships. The decision was made after the Centre accepted an expert panel's recommendations.

National Affairs.title: No murder, affair proof: SC clears woman of killing husband after 19 years
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has exonerated a woman accused of killing her husband after 19 years, ruling that suspicion alone is not enough to prove guilt. The court's decision highlights the importance of evidence-based proof in the Indian justice system. This case sets a precedent for future cases where suspicion may not be enough to secure a conviction.

National Affairs.title: Can an ex parte hearing decide someone's fate as a foreigner?
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that fundamental rights such as equality and the right to life and liberty apply to all individuals within India, regardless of their citizenship status. This decision has significant implications for foreigners living in India and ensures that their rights are protected. The court's interpretation of 'any person' within India is a crucial aspect of this ruling.

National Affairs.title: SC upholds Lalu's bail in fodder scam
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has upheld the bail granted to former Bihar Chief Minister Lalu Yadav in the fodder scam case, directing the high court to expedite the hearing of pending appeals. This decision allows Lalu Yadav to remain out of jail while the case is ongoing. The Supreme Court's decision is significant as it sets a precedent for similar cases involving high-profile individuals.

National Affairs.title: How Parliament overturned court rulings before
National Affairs.summary: The Indian Parliament has a history of overturning court rulings through legislation, with the recent CAPF Act being an example. This act overturns a Supreme Court ruling on officers' promotions, highlighting the complex relationship between the judiciary and the legislature. The article provides insight into the instances where Parliament has reversed judgments, showcasing the dynamic between the two branches of government.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court asks Telangana to cure defects in plea against Polavaram project
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has rejected a petition filed by the Telangana government against the construction of the Polavaram project on the Godavari river, citing defects in the plea. The court has asked Telangana to rectify these defects before proceeding with the case. This decision is significant as it affects the ongoing dispute between Telangana and Andhra Pradesh over the project.
